title: Oxymeris foveolata
taxonomy: Oxymeris foveolata is classified as a species of sea snail, a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Terebridae, which are commonly known as auger snails. These snails are characterized by their elongated shells with a narrow aperture and a distinctive pattern of ridges and grooves. The classification of Oxymeris foveolata places it within the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and order Neogastropoda. The family Terebridae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Oxymeris foveolata being a member of the subfamily Terebrinae. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among these snails.
